last_item: Second Mode-S convergence check (backlog still actionable-empty: same NEEDS-BACKEND backup-badge item + two low/watch items, none promoted). Ran a fresh 5-agent parallel discovery panel covering every view + nav/IA + first-run + cross-view consistency, each given the full already-shipped/already-rejected digest and a deliberately HIGH bar (genuinely new, high-or-med-payoff, non-over-design, frontend-only, one-view-fixable only). Coverage identical to iter 15 for comparability.
last_result: CONVERGED (2nd consecutive) — all 5 agents independently returned 'NO new high/med item'. Coverage: (1) Dashboard+Query, (2) Roles&DBs+Backups+BackupStorageForm, (3) Alerts+Migrate, (4) Settings+DatabaseTuning+Pooler+Login, (5) nav/IA+first-run+cross-view consistency. Agent 1 re-examined the Query zero-rows state + speculative write-SQL detector (neither high-payoff); agent 3 re-probed the two iter-15 nits and confirmed both already-handled/zero-payoff (cluster 'Exclude' already HAS a FieldDescription in Migrate.tsx; Alerts 'Send test' uses a standard native disabled `title`, not the iter-8 decorator-tooltip pattern); agents 2/4/5 found mature, internally-consistent views (graduated destructive gates, honest state, co-located backup config, paired empty-states+hints, plain-English copy). No code shipped, docs-only commit, no hard gates needed. Per the contract this is the SECOND stable_streak increment -> 2/3. ONE more clean convergence pass -> write COMPLETE.md and stop the loop. Next iteration: run the final Mode-S convergence check; do NOT manufacture low-value work to avoid converging (converging early is a win).
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
